title: Paraphlomis rugosa
intro: Paraphlomis rugosa, also known as "Rough Flom" or "Paraphlomis", is a type of perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to China and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues such as fever, respiratory infections, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Paraphlomis rugosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Paraphlomis rugosa is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
